title: Specialist Team Removes HMS Victory Masts in Nighttime Conservation Operation

contents:
## Specialist Team Removes HMS Victory Masts in Nighttime Conservation Operation

### Overview
Specialist team deploys 750-tonne crane over three nights. Crew extracts foremast, mizzen, and bowsprit from HMS Victory. Project advances £42m restoration at Portsmouth Historic Dockyard.

### Event Details
Specialist team executes nighttime operation to remove HMS Victory's remaining masts. Crew deploys 750-tonne crane across three nights for precise extraction. Technicians lower foremast, mizzen mast, and bowsprit with meticulous care. Workers position masts near ship for immediate restoration. Project forms part of £42m conservation effort launched after months of planning. Team coordinates 30-strong personnel to handle complex disassembly. Efforts preserve Royal Navy's iconic warship, flagship at Trafalgar battle. Dockyard staff prepares masts for detailed repair and preservation. Digital archaeologists capture 3D data during process. Conservators uncover 260 years of historical layers. Operation demonstrates commitment to safeguarding naval heritage. Portsmouth Historic Dockyard serves as central hub. Crew works through nights to minimize disruption. Technicians employ advanced tools for accuracy. Project advances HMS Victory toward full restoration. Public follows progress through updates. Heritage experts highlight significance of masts in ship's structure. Team reports successful initial removals. Efforts continue to protect vessel from decay. National Museum of the Royal Navy oversees aspects. Specialists train for unique challenges. Operation showcases expertise in historic ship conservation.

### Source Context
@NatlHistShips (National Historic Ships) covers this category of developments. The account focuses on royal navy historic warship decommission preservation naval vessel.
